query: dog parks in Seattle with fenced off-leash areas

results.name: I-5 Colonnade Off-Leash Area
results.fenced_quote: This is NOT an enclosed dog park. City needs to change the sign or actually make it an enclosed dog park.
results.features_quote: I-5 Colonnade park is located on a steep slope under the I-5 freeway just north of downtown Seattle and covers .5 acre with several large tiers connected by walkways and some stairs. It has a crushed gravel surface, several benches, and a potable water source at the north entrance.
results.summary: I-5 Colonnade is an official Seattle Parks off-leash area tucked on a steep, tiered half-acre slope under the I-5 freeway in Eastlake. It offers all-weather cover from rain, wood-chip and gravel surfacing, logs and rocks, benches and native plantings on the adjacent hillside, but the site is not fully enclosed by a continuous perimeter fence and users report dogs can escape toward the freeway edge.
results.backboard_quote: This is NOT an enclosed dog park. City needs to change the sign or actually make it an enclosed dog park.

results.name: Jose Rizal Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: The fenced-in four-acre park offers spectacular views of the city and Puget Sound, a walking trail and drinking water for pups.
results.features_quote: The four-acre off-leash dog park at Dr. Jose Rizal Park was opened in 2001. The fenced dog areas are accessed from a long set of stairs at the north end of the Park.
results.summary: Dr. Jose Rizal Park's off-leash area is a 4-acre fenced off-leash area on the north end of Beacon Hill with sweeping views of Puget Sound and downtown Seattle. Entry is via a double-gated staircase at the north end of the park. The area has a walking trail, drinking water and open space, though reviews note it can be muddy and has had trash and maintenance problems.
results.backboard_quote: The fenced-in four-acre park offers spectacular views of the city and Puget Sound, a walking trail and drinking water for pups.

results.name: Regrade Park
results.fenced_quote: There is a 5-foot-tall fence that encloses the entire park, and there are double 'airlock' gates at each entrance to ensure your dog's safety.
results.features_quote: Regrade Park in Belltown is a 0.3-acre urban pocket park with concrete and mulch, ringed in cyclone fencing. The park has double-gated entry/exit and five-foot-tall fencing to assure your dog's safety near substantial street traffic, and it offers running water and wheelchair accessibility.
results.summary: Regrade Park is a 0.3-acre official Seattle Parks off-leash area in the heart of Belltown at 3rd and Bell. It is fully enclosed by a 5-foot cyclone fence with double 'airlock' gates at each entrance, concrete and mulch surfacing, and running water. It is open 6 a.m. to 10 p.m.
results.backboard_quote: There is a 5-foot-tall fence that encloses the entire park, and there are double 'airlock' gates at each entrance to ensure your dog's safety.

results.name: Genesee Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: The dog park is entirely fenced-in with two double-gated entrances, a doggie drinking fountain and a separate small/shy dog area.
results.features_quote: The Genesee off-leash area is 2.7 acres and is completely fenced with two double-gated entrances. The center 2 acres of the off-leash area is covered in gravel, which makes it mud-free in the winter.
results.summary: Genesee Park's off-leash area is a 2.7-acre official Seattle Parks OLA in Southeast Seattle, just west of Seward Park. It is completely enclosed by waist-high chain-link fencing with two double-gated entrances, features a gravel center that stays mud-free in winter, a doggie drinking fountain, and a separate small/shy dog area added in early 2018.
results.backboard_quote: The dog park is entirely fenced-in with two double-gated entrances, a doggie drinking fountain and a separate small/shy dog area.

results.name: Woodland Park Off-Leash Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: Woodland Park offers a fully enclosed off-leash area, providing a safe environment for dogs to run and play without worry.
results.features_quote: The fully fenced space is gravel covered in a fully-shaded, wooded area with a sloped hillside and steps. The dog park itself consists of two sections, one for larger dogs and another for smaller dogs.
results.summary: Woodland Park Off-Leash Area is a 1-acre official Seattle Parks OLA in the Phinney Ridge/Green Lake area. It is fully fenced and gravel-surfaced in a shaded, wooded hillside with steps, and is split into separate sections for large and small dogs. Amenities include benches, running water, waste stations and nearby restrooms.
results.backboard_quote: Woodland Park offers a fully enclosed off-leash area, providing a safe environment for dogs to run and play without worry.

results.name: Magnuson Park Off-Leash Area
results.address: 7400 Sand Point Way NE, Seattle, WA 98115
results.fenced_quote: The Magnuson off-leash area contains 8.6 acres and is Seattle's biggest fully-fenced back yard for canines.
results.features_quote: The site has a large, generally flat play area, a winding trail with several open areas and changes of scenery along the way... This off-leash area is the only one inside city limits with water access (Lake Washington's freshwater shoreline). This off-leash area has a small and shy dog area within the larger dog park.
results.summary: Warren G. Magnuson Park Off-Leash Area is Seattle's largest official OLA at 8.6 fully fenced acres, located along Lake Washington's shoreline at Sand Point. It is the only off-leash area inside Seattle city limits with water access (the city's only dog beach), plus a large flat play area, winding compact-gravel trails (wheelchair accessible), a separate small/shy dog area near the main kiosk, shaded benches and numerous waste stations. Stewarded by the Magnuson Off-Leash Group (MOLG). Note: the OSM coordinate 47.6912, -122.4004 appears inaccurate; the actual OLA is at approximately 47.683, -122.248.
results.backboard_quote: The Magnuson off-leash area contains 8.6 acres and is Seattle's biggest fully-fenced back yard for canines.

results.name: Magnolia Manor Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: The city-run and fully-fenced Magnolia Manor Park Off-Leash Area is perched atop one of Seattle's many hills near downtown at the site of a covered reservoir. Two security gates safeguard the main play area and another one protects the small/shy dog section.
results.features_quote: The park functions as a 21,000 sq. ft off leash area with a 'chuck it' game zone and dog drinking fountain. The park offers ground surfaces of primarily small round rocks and bark chips, allowing room for dogs to run at full speed and play fetch.
results.summary: Magnolia Manor Park Off-Leash Area is an official 21,000-sq-ft Seattle Parks OLA sitting atop a covered reservoir in Magnolia. The entire site is surrounded by a chain-link fence with two security gates protecting the main play area and a third gate for a separate fenced small/shy dog section. Surfaces are small round rocks and bark chips; amenities include a dog drinking fountain, poop bags, and a 'chuck it' fetch zone.
results.backboard_quote: The city-run and fully-fenced Magnolia Manor Park Off-Leash Area is perched atop one of Seattle's many hills near downtown at the site of a covered reservoir. Two security gates safeguard the main play area and another one protects the small/shy dog section.

results.name: Westcrest Small Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: A special, separated area for small and shy dogs is located on the southwest side of the main off-leash area.
results.features_quote: From the parking lot, follow the path to the right of the playground and picnic area. Small or shy dogs that want to avoid bigger pups can romp in their own fenced section.
results.summary: This is the separate small/shy dog section of Westcrest Park's official Seattle Parks OLA, located on the southwest side of the main off-leash area. It is its own fenced enclosure carved out of the 8.4-acre Westcrest OLA so that small or shy dogs can play away from bigger pups.
results.backboard_quote: A special, separated area for small and shy dogs is located on the southwest side of the main off-leash area.

results.name: Westcrest Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: The park is fully fenced, ensuring that your dog can explore and play freely in the designated off-leash area.
results.features_quote: Westcrest Park Off-Leash Area is an 8.4 acres, dog-friendly park in Seattle, WA. The park features a doggie drinking fountain, shade, trees and lots of open space. For people, the off-leash area provides benches, chairs, and a shady place to relax.
results.summary: Westcrest Park Off-Leash Area is Seattle's second-largest official OLA at about 8.4 acres in West Seattle's Highland Park neighborhood. The main area is fully fenced with open meadow, paths, shade trees, a doggie drinking fountain and an obstacle/agility course, plus a separately fenced small/shy dog section on the southwest side. Dogs must be leashed between the parking lot and the OLA.
results.backboard_quote: The park is fully fenced, ensuring that your dog can explore and play freely in the designated off-leash area.

results.name: South Lake Union Dog Park
results.features_quote: Denny Park is located at 100 Dexter Ave N in the South Lake Union neighborhood and is a 0.105-acre off-leash area... The area features a 4-foot fence with double-gated entrance and has a granolithic gravel surface.
results.summary: The OSM node labeled 'South Lake Union Dog Park' at this coordinate does not correspond to a named Seattle Parks off-leash area; no authoritative source lists an OLA with this exact name. The nearest official OLA is Denny Park Off-Leash Area at 100 Dexter Ave N, which is fenced; this OSM entry may be a duplicate reference or a private/informal plaza dog area, so fencing cannot be confirmed from authoritative sources.

results.name: Day 1 Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: The cozy space is fenced and is mostly concrete except a small turf pad, with lots of lush plants around the edges and benches that you sort of lean on.
results.features_quote: You can enter from 6th Avenue through the publicly-accessible walkway and you'll find the 1,000-square-foot off-leash area. The park features a tiny fenced area with leaning benches inside and real seating outside, with a water hose and fountain to keep things functional.
results.summary: Amazon - Day 1 Dog Park is a privately owned but publicly accessible 1,000-sq-ft off-leash area adjacent to Amazon's Day 1 building and The Spheres at 2112 6th Ave. It is a small, fenced urban dog park mostly surfaced in concrete with a small turf pad, leaning benches, and a water hose/fountain. It is designed for quick downtown dog breaks rather than long runs.
results.backboard_quote: The cozy space is fenced and is mostly concrete except a small turf pad, with lots of lush plants around the edges and benches that you sort of lean on.

results.name: Blue Dog Pond - Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: It is fully fenced and has running water.
results.features_quote: Blue Dog Pond in Southeast Seattle near I-90 is 1.7 acres and is a wide, rectangular field perfect for throwing balls with grassy side slopes that your dog can run up and down. There are interesting art sculptures throughout the park that make it unique, including a giant reposing 'blue dog' at the entrance.
results.summary: Blue Dog Pond is a 1.7-acre official Seattle Parks off-leash area in southeast Seattle near I-90, adjacent to Judkins Park. It is fully fenced with running water, a large flat gravel play area, grassy sloped sides and a walking trail. As a stormwater catchment, it can get muddy in the rainy season. It is known for its giant blue-dog art sculpture at the entrance.
results.backboard_quote: It is fully fenced and has running water.

results.name: Kinnear Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: The off-leash area is surrounded by a 4' fence and has a double gate entrance with a single gate emergency exit in the rear to ensure dog safety.
results.features_quote: The dog park is 5,400 square feet, on a relatively flat incline beneath a towering forest, and features a kiosk, natural wooden fencing, wood chip surfacing, logs, rocks, a bench and native plantings on the adjacent hillside. The park offers views westward over Elliott Bay.
results.summary: Lower Kinnear Park Off-Leash Area is an official 5,400-sq-ft Seattle Parks OLA tucked into the lower section of Kinnear Park on the west side of Queen Anne. It is surrounded by a 4-foot natural wood fence with a double-gated entrance and a rear emergency gate, and features wood-chip surfacing, logs, rocks, a bench, kiosk and Elliott Bay views. Access is via a short nature trail from the west end of Roy St.
results.backboard_quote: The off-leash area is surrounded by a 4' fence and has a double gate entrance with a single gate emergency exit in the rear to ensure dog safety.

results.name: Denny Substation Dog Park
results.fenced_quote: Denny Substation Dog Park, located in Seattle, WA, provides a well-maintained, fully fenced, off-leash area designed to offer dogs a safe, enjoyable space to play.
results.features_quote: The public benefits include 44,000 square feet of open space that activates the neighborhood including an off-leash dog park and a 0.25-mile walking loop. The off-leash dog park is designed to be well fenced and have play features for canines, with boulders acting as play features while foliage lines the edges of the space.
results.summary: Denny Substation Dog Park is a Seattle City Light-operated off-leash area within the Denny Substation civic space in South Lake Union. It is part of 44,000 sq ft of public open space that includes a 0.25-mile walking loop. The dog area is fully fenced with double-gated entry, granolithic gravel surfacing, boulders as play features and foliage edging. Open 5 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily. Note: this is a City Light project, not a Seattle Parks OLA.
results.backboard_quote: Denny Substation Dog Park, located in Seattle, WA, provides a well-maintained, fully fenced, off-leash area designed to offer dogs a safe, enjoyable space to play.

results.name: Dog Yard Bar
results.fenced_quote: The entire park is surrounded with six to eight foot fencing so that dogs could not go over or under. Additionally, you not only have to enter a door to get into the main lobby but to get into the dog areas are two more secured gates.
results.features_quote: The off-leash park features a large turf zone, boulders, tunnels, and more. If it's hot out, they have misters and water features to splash in. When it's cold, they start up outdoor heaters.
results.summary: Dog Yard Bar is a private 21+ indoor/outdoor off-leash dog play park combined with a bar in Ballard. It is not a public park but it is fully enclosed: the perimeter has 6-to-8-foot fencing and entry requires passing through a lobby door plus two additional secured gates. Features include a large turf zone, boulders, tunnels, misters and water features, outdoor heaters. Dogs must have a Pet Profile and uploaded vaccination records.
results.backboard_quote: The entire park is surrounded with six to eight foot fencing so that dogs could not go over or under. Additionally, you not only have to enter a door to get into the main lobby but to get into the dog areas are two more secured gates.

results.name: Construction Site Dog Park
results.address: Near 22nd Ave and E Cherry St, Seattle, WA (Central District)
results.fenced_quote: After neighbors asked if they could bring their dogs there, a fence was placed around the area.
results.features_quote: A developer opened a pop-up dog park at a property near 22nd and Cherry in Seattle's Central District... This pop-up dog park was opened by an area developer on property awaiting future construction. The developer behind this initiative is Benjamin Maritz, a multifamily housing developer, dog owner, and long-time Seattle resident.
results.summary: 'Construction Site Dog Park' is a privately-run, informal pop-up off-leash dog park at a vacant development parcel near 22nd and Cherry in Seattle's Central District, created by developer Benjamin Maritz on land awaiting future construction. It is not a City of Seattle OLA. A perimeter fence was installed around the lot when the space opened as a de facto dog park, so it is enclosed, though long-term status depends on when redevelopment starts.
results.backboard_quote: After neighbors asked if they could bring their dogs there, a fence was placed around the area.

results.name: Dogwood Play Park
results.fenced_quote: There is an expansive indoor and outdoor play space for off-leash play separated into small and large dog areas, and separate areas (indoors and out) for active play and gentle play. The facility is completely fenced.
results.features_quote: Dogwood Play Park is Seattle's original indoor/outdoor off-leash dog park and bar. Dogs can romp around both the indoor and outdoor areas which are equipped with play structures such as tunnels and giant oversized tires.
results.summary: Dogwood Play Park is a private 21+ indoor/outdoor off-leash dog play park and bar in Seattle's Lake City neighborhood, off 125th St and Lake City Way. It is completely fenced and separated into small- and large-dog zones with distinct active-play and gentle-play areas. Amenities include tunnels, oversized-tire play structures, a bar with beer/cider/wine and snacks. Entry is $16 drop-in or a membership, and current Rabies/Bordetella/DHPP records are required.
results.backboard_quote: There is an expansive indoor and outdoor play space for off-leash play separated into small and large dog areas, and separate areas (indoors and out) for active play and gentle play. The facility is completely fenced.

results.name: Growlerz Seattle
results.fenced_quote: They have created a spacious and secure facility where dogs can have plenty of space to run, play, and explore.
results.features_quote: The facility offers doggy day care, play park, and dog park taproom services. Dogs have the opportunity to play in the outdoor play area and swim in the pool. The facility provides heaters and covered areas, ensuring comfort year-round.
results.summary: Growlerz Seattle is a private dog daycare, play park, and taproom at 5269 Rainier Ave S in the Columbia City/Hillman City area. It is a commercial facility (not a public park) with an outdoor play area, pool, indoor covered areas and heaters, operating as a secure enclosed dog-play venue for paying customers. It is not a Seattle Parks OLA.
results.backboard_quote: They have created a spacious and secure facility where dogs can have plenty of space to run, play, and explore.
